metadata.description: Executable TDD plan for FDA readiness improvements derived from three research reports. Focus: (1) wire OpenLineage into Prefect flows for real, (2) CycloneDX SBOM + Grype, (3) KG updates for MedMLOps/agentic/SBOM, (4) GitHub issues for deferred work (UX monitoring, federated learning, commercial compliance). Uses self-learning-iterative-coder skill.

related_issues.issue: FDA-ready test set documentation and lineage tracking
OpenLineage/Marquez integration for all Prefect flows

phase.name: openlineage-flow-wiring
phase.summary:
Wire LineageEmitter.pipeline_run() into all 5 core Prefect flows. Currently LineageEmitter exists (271 LOC) with full OpenLineage v2 event model but ZERO flows call it. Tests exist for isolated emission but not for flow-level integration.
ALSO: Wire AuditTrail.log_test_evaluation() into analysis_flow (eval) and add cumulative eval/test_set_access_count MLflow metric. This is the $100k+ FDA test-set-reuse gap from Issue #821.

task.title: Test: train_flow emits OpenLineage START/COMPLETE events
task.description: Add test class TestTrainFlowEmitsLineageInFlow that patches LineageEmitter and asserts train_flow calls pipeline_run() with correct job_name="train-flow" and input/output datasets. Current test file tests emission in isolation — this tests the WIRING.
task.acceptance: Test fails because train_flow.py does not import LineageEmitter.

task.title: Wire LineageEmitter into train_flow.py
task.description: Import LineageEmitter. Wrap the flow body with: emitter = LineageEmitter(namespace="minivess") with emitter.pipeline_run("train-flow", inputs=[{"namespace": "minivess", "name": "raw_volumes"}], outputs=[{"namespace": "minivess", "name": "checkpoints"}]): # existing flow body ~5-10 lines added. Zero DevEx impact.
task.acceptance: T1 test passes. No existing tests broken.

task.title: Wire LineageEmitter into post_training_flow.py
task.description: Same pattern as T2. job_name="post-training-flow", inputs=checkpoints, outputs=swa_checkpoints. Add test in test_flow_lineage_emission.py.
task.acceptance: Lineage events emitted on flow run.

task.title: Wire LineageEmitter into analysis_flow.py (eval flow)
task.description: Same pattern. job_name="analysis-flow", inputs=checkpoints+test_datasets, outputs=metrics+ensemble_models. CRITICAL: Also wire AuditTrail.log_test_evaluation() when test split is evaluated. Add cumulative eval/test_set_access_count MLflow metric. This addresses Issue #821 test-set firewall.
task.acceptance: Lineage events emitted. AuditTrail.log_test_evaluation() called for each test dataset evaluation. MLflow metric logged.

task.title: Wire LineageEmitter into biostatistics_flow.py
task.description: Same pattern. job_name="biostatistics-flow", inputs=mlflow_runs, outputs=figures+tables+lineage_manifest. Note: biostatistics_flow already has its own lineage manifest (biostatistics_lineage.py) — OpenLineage events are ADDITIONAL, wrapping the entire flow execution.
task.acceptance: OpenLineage events emitted alongside existing lineage manifest.

task.title: Wire LineageEmitter into deploy_flow.py
task.description: Same pattern. job_name="deploy-flow", inputs=champion_checkpoint, outputs=bentoml_model+onnx_export. deploy_flow already has audit_trails list — wire AuditTrail class (from compliance/audit.py) to replace ad-hoc dicts.

task.title: Run make test-staging — all lineage tests pass
task.acceptance: Zero failures. All new + existing lineage tests green.

phase.guardrails: - LineageEmitter import at top of each flow file - pipeline_run() wraps entire flow body as context manager - No existing flow logic modified — only wrapping added - AuditTrail.log_test_evaluation() ONLY in analysis_flow for test splits - eval/test_set_access_count is cumulative MLflow metric

phase.name: sbom-cyclonedx-grype

new-issue.title: CycloneDX SBOM generation + Grype vulnerability scanning

phase.summary: Add Python-level SBOM generation via cyclonedx-bom (reads uv.lock natively in v7.2.2) alongside existing Docker-level Trivy SBOM. Add Grype for vulnerability scanning of the generated SBOM. Update Makefile with new targets. Resolve KG sbom_generation node.

task.title: Test: make sbom-python generates CycloneDX JSON from uv environment
task.description: Test that cyclonedx-py can be invoked and produces valid CycloneDX JSON with expected fields (bomFormat, specVersion, components). Test fails because cyclonedx-bom not yet installed.
task.acceptance: Test imports cyclonedx and validates JSON schema.

task.title: Add cyclonedx-bom dependency + Makefile targets
task.description: 1. uv add --group compliance cyclonedx-bom 2. Add Makefile targets: sbom-python: generates sbom/python-sbom.json via cyclonedx-py sbom-scan: runs grype on sbom/python-sbom.json sbom-all: sbom + sbom-python (Docker + Python) 3. Note: grype is a binary (not pip), install instruction in Makefile
task.acceptance: make sbom-python produces valid CycloneDX JSON. T8 passes.

task.title: Resolve sbom_generation KG node to cyclonedx_plus_grype
task.description: Update status: not_started → resolved. resolved_option: cyclonedx_plus_grype. Add resolution_evidence pointing to Makefile targets and FDA Section 524B mandate. Update posteriors.

phase.guardrails: - cyclonedx-bom in [compliance] optional group, not core deps - SBOM output to sbom/ directory (gitignored, generated artifact) - Existing make sbom (Trivy/Docker) unchanged - grype is external binary, not Python package

phase.name: kg-updates
phase.summary: Update knowledge graph with MedMLOps 4-pillar framework, agentic stack decisions, and SBOM resolution. These are documentation-only changes that inform future implementation decisions.

task.title: Add MedMLOps 4-pillar framework to manuscript domain
task.description:
Add MedMLOps framework (de Almeida et al. 2025, European Radiology) as a key reference. Map our flows to the 4 pillars: 1. Availability → Docker + SkyPilot + Prefect 2. Continuous monitoring → Evidently + drift flow + OpenLineage 3. Privacy/data protection → DVC + opt-in architecture + federated (future) 4. Ease of use → Hydra config + zero-manual-work principle
This is manuscript novelty: we demonstrate all 4 pillars in a preclinical neuroscience MLOps platform.

task.title: Add agentic stack decision node
task.description: NEW decision node. Status: partial. Options: pydantic_ai (current), langgraph (planned), copilotkit_ag_ui (planned). Document existing Pydantic AI stubs in pyproject.toml. Note CopilotKit/AG-UI + WebMCP for dashboard/annotation agentic UI. Update architecture domain to include agentic_orchestration. Update navigator.yaml decisions list for architecture domain.

task.title: Update operations domain with CISO/compliance tooling
task.description: Add ciso_assistant decision stub (not_started). Update sbom_generation to reference resolved cyclonedx_plus_grype. Add MedMLOps reference to the domain description.

phase.name: github-issues
phase.summary: Create GitHub issues for work that is planned but deferred. Each issue has clear scope, acceptance criteria, and priority.

task.title: Create P1 issue: Agentic stack — CopilotKit/AG-UI + WebMCP + Pydantic AI for dashboard
task.body: Install CopilotKit (AG-UI protocol) + WebMCP on dashboard/annotation module. Integrate with existing Pydantic AI micro-orchestration stubs. Add A2UI (Google) for declarative generative UI. References: docs/planning/langgraph-agents-plan.md, music-attribution-scaffold repo for AG-UI patterns. KG: knowledge-graph/decisions/L3-technology/agentic_orchestration.yaml

task.title: Create P2 issue: UX monitoring stubs — Sentry + PostHog (opt-in telemetry)
task.body: Bare minimum stubs for: - sentry-sdk: error tracking for all Python entry points - posthog-python: product analytics for dashboard (opt-in only) PostHog is SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA-ready with BAA (May 2025). Self-hosted Docker deployment aligns with our Docker-per-flow arch. Both tools behind OPT_IN_TELEMETRY env var (default: disabled).

task.title: Create P2 issue: Federated learning — NVIDIA FLARE vs MONAI FL comparison
task.body: Compare federated learning frameworks for multi-site neurovascular imaging data pooling: - NVIDIA FLARE v2.7.0 (dominant, Mass General/NCI deployments) - MONAI FL (https://monai.readthedocs.io/en/1.3.2/fl.html) — MONAI-native, better fit for our MONAI-first principle - Flower: general-purpose, likely NOT suitable for medical imaging Integration point: ModelAdapter ABC for site-local training. Not on publication gate pathway — research exploration only.

task.title: Create P2 issue: CISO Assistant Community for compliance CI
task.body: Evaluate https://github.com/intuitem/ciso-assistant-community for automated compliance checking in CI. Compare with CycloneDX+Grype (which we're implementing in Phase 2): - CISO Assistant: GRC framework (SOC2, ISO 27001, NIST) — policy-level - CycloneDX+Grype: SBOM+vuln scanning — dependency-level They are complementary, not alternatives. Note: GitHub Actions CI is DISABLED (credits). Would need workflow_dispatch. Academic project — commercial compliance (Oneleet/Vanta) is deferred until commercial use case emerges.

task.title: Create P2 issue: Commercial compliance pathway (Oneleet over Vanta)
task.body: For future commercial deployment: evaluate Oneleet ($25-40k/yr) over Vanta ($7.5-20k/yr) for genuine security vs "compliance theater" (Oneleet: OSCP-certified pen testing, vCISO, real security controls). NOT needed for academic project. Create and close immediately as documentation of the decision to defer. Reference: docs/planning/fda-insights-second-pass.md Section 2.5.

step.description: Wire OpenLineage into all 5 Prefect flows (P0)
Create GitHub issues for deferred work

out-of-scope.item: Marquez PostgreSQL wiring — deferred to after factorial experiment
PCCP YAML template — deferred to after factorial experiment
Sentry/PostHog implementation — P2 issue only, stubs deferred
Federated learning — P2 research issue only
CopilotKit/AG-UI implementation — P1 issue only, code deferred
CISO Assistant CI — P2 issue only
ISO 14971 risk management — deferred to clinical transition
21 CFR Part 11 electronic signatures — deferred to clinical transition
Threat model document — deferred to after factorial experiment
QMSR production controls documentation — deferred, simple .md file
Ralph Loop monitoring — NOT needed for this plan (straightforward tasks)
